commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=69844e17a4d3baf138b1e25f619dff729a011b87)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a default-disabled structured `core/doctor/memory-search` Doctor lint check plus read-only provider-specific auth-profile source detection and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +175, Tests +179. Total +354 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR finding at source level: current `main` emits the QMD session-export note after the binary probe, while PR head returns before that config-only note when selected lint skips QMD probing. I did not run a live latest-head CLI repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR has a narrow code defect, but it also needs contributor-refreshed real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the auth-profile lint semantics before merge.

target.title: doctor: add memory search lint findings
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds `core/doctor/memory-search` as a structured Doctor lint finding source.
- Reuses the existing memory-search doctor note logic through an injected note sink so legacy `openclaw doctor` output stays unchanged.
- Keeps the new structured check opt-in/default-off: it is available through `--only core/doctor/memory-search` or `--all`, but plain `doctor --lint` does not run it.
- Keeps lint read-only/safe: skips informational disabled-memory notes, treats unprobed key-optional readiness as skipped, suppresses configured QMD binary probing, and avoids auth-profile/OAuth resolution from lint detection.
- Fixes the selected-lint auth-profile false positive: when auth-profile sources exist but profile resolution is intentionally skipped, memory-search lint stays quiet instead of reporting a definitive missing API key.

## Contract / Safety
- No plugin SDK/public contract changes (`plugin-sdk:surface:check` and `plugin-sdk:check-exports` clean).
- No repair behavior or data model change.
- Default `openclaw doctor --lint` behavior is unchanged for memory search because `core/doctor/memory-search` has `defaultEnabled: false`.
- `openclaw doctor --lint --only core/doctor/memory-search` reports config/env findings from read-only config/env state only; it does not execute configured QMD commands or refresh/persist auth profiles.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Default-disabled structured Doctor lint for memory search, including selected missing-key warnings, default-off behavior, QMD no-spawn behavior, and the auth-profile-backed credential false-positive fix.

Real environment tested:
WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 source checkout at `3b2269258399bfe4914781b5378f81bd2e64d23d`, using isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` temp directories. Provider key env vars were unset for the proof commands. QMD proof used a marker-writing fake QMD command to prove lint did not spawn it.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `pnpm -s openclaw doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/memory-search` with memory search enabled for OpenAI and no OpenAI API key.
2. `pnpm -s openclaw doctor --lint --json` on the same config to prove default lint does not include `core/doctor/memory-search`.
3. `pnpm -s openclaw doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/memory-search` with `memory.backend=qmd` and `memory.qmd.command` pointed at a marker-writing script.
4. `pnpm -s openclaw doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/memory-search` with the same missing-key config plus an isolated auth-profile source file at the default agent dir.

Evidence after fix:

```text
## selected-missing-key
exit=1
{
  "totalFindings": 1,
  "memoryFindings": 1,
  "firstMemory": {
    "checkId": "core/doctor/memory-search",
    "severity": "warning",
    "path": "agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider",
    "target": null,
    "fixHint": "Semantic recall will not work without a valid API key.\n\n\nFix (pick one):\n- Set OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment\n- Configure credentials: openclaw configure --section model\n- To disable: openclaw config set agents.defaults.memorySearch.enabled false\n\nVerify: openclaw memory status --deep"
  }
}

## default-off
exit=1
{
  "totalFindings": 34,
  "memoryFindings": 0,
  "firstMemory": {
    "checkId": null,
    "severity": null,
    "path": null,
    "target": null,
    "fixHint": null
  }
}

## qmd-no-spawn
exit=0
{
  "totalFindings": 0,
  "memoryFindings": 0,
  "firstMemory": {
    "checkId": null,
    "severity": null,
    "path": null,
    "target": null,
    "fixHint": null
  }
}

## auth-profile-source-skipped
exit=0
{
  "totalFindings": 0,
  "memoryFindings": 0,
  "firstMemory": {
    "checkId": null,
    "severity": null,
    "path": null,
    "target": null,
    "fixHint": null
  }
}

QMD_MARKER_EXISTS=no
```

Observed result after fix:
Selected `core/doctor/memory-search` reports the expected missing-key warning only when config/env/auth-profile source state cannot satisfy credentials. Plain default lint reports zero memory-search findings. QMD selected lint exits cleanly and does not spawn the configured QMD command. An auth-profile source keeps selected lint quiet without resolving or mutating auth profiles.

What was not tested:
Packaged installer/runtime proof. This is source CLI proof plus focused unit/type/lint coverage for a default-off Doctor lint detector.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (2 shards, 106 tests)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-memory-search.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/doctor-memory-search.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:check-exports`
- `git diff --check`

## Not Tested
- Packaged installer/runtime proof; this is source CLI + focused unit/type/lint coverage for a default-off Doctor lint detector.
target.author: giodl73-repo
